codeReview.summary: I reviewed the feature metadata, the worker transcript skeleton, the checkpoint handoff, the `milestone-shareout-worker` procedure, and the branch state around HEAD `0630486d940660655ac75f17f61726b5f67eed53`. This is a no-source-change checkpoint feature: the referenced commit is the already-sealed `validate(batch-pack-hard-cut): seal user testing proof` commit, and the reviewed scope is the shareout handoff rather than a new implementation diff. The handoff captures the required branch state (`git status`, commits since `origin/main`, and diff stat versus `origin/main`), points to the sealed batch-pack scrutiny and user-testing artifacts, and returns control to the orchestrator as required. I found no blocking or non-blocking defects in the reviewed feature scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `milestone-shareout-worker` leaves room for redundant revalidation during checkpoint-only features. This worker re-ran `.factory/init.sh` and `bash scripts/gate_fast.sh` even though the skill only needs branch/shareout evidence and `.factory/services.yaml` already provides a dedicated `milestone-shareout` command.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/milestone-shareout-worker/SKILL.md:31-37` says to run the shareout/review commands from `.factory/services.yaml`; `.factory/services.yaml:42-43` already defines `commands.milestone-shareout`; but `handoffs/2026-03-25T09-01-27-122Z__checkpoint-push-batch-pack-hard-cut__b0556829-a7d1-4e63-a7bf-5e1e29b2d78d.json:21-38` shows the worker ran `.factory/init.sh`, `bash scripts/gate_fast.sh`, and separate git status/log/diff commands instead.

summary: Pass: the reviewed feature correctly acted as a no-source-change checkpoint shareout at HEAD `0630486d940660655ac75f17f61726b5f67eed53`, returning the required branch/validation evidence so the orchestrator can push for remote review before dispatch-contract work.
